facts.claim: 27 rules total, three coexisting analgosedation pathway implementations (v3 automatico=wired production, v1 automatico=legacy/superseded, manual=nurse-entered) each with its own 12/4/6-criterion set and its own VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO alert aggregator (RULE-SEDACAO-014, -023, -021 respectively) — no single canonical scoring model.

Only 4 of the 12 v3 predicates (criterio_1, 5, 7, 8) are actually wired into calcular_criterios/calcular_alerta_v2; criterio_3,4,6,9,10,11,12 are defined-but-unwired dead code, several containing latent logic bugs that are inert only because nothing calls them.

Continuous-sedative overdose/weaning thresholds are expressed in ml/h infusion volume (e.g. midazolam>=15, propofol>=20, fentanil>=15, cetamina>=15, dexmedetomidina>=15 ml/h) with no published clinical anchor — guideline dosing is weight-based (mcg/kg/min or mg/kg/h) and conversion depends on institution-specific drug concentration, so these numbers cannot be ported as clinical thresholds without a concentration table.

Pain assessment uses a dual-scale model: VISUAL analog 0-10 with moderate=4-6/severe=7-10, or COMPORTAMENTAL (Behavioral Pain Scale) 3-12 with moderate=7-9/severe=10-12; both require the condition true on two consecutive fluid-balance records to fire (RULE-SEDACAO-001/002), matching SCCM PADIS 2018 pain bands and BPS (Payen 2001).

RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale) is used throughout as both an int field (-5..+4, evolucao.rass) and a str-enum field (manual model, values like '-2'..'-5'); deep sedation is RASS -3 to -5, light-sedation target is RASS 0 to -2 per PADIS 2018.

Sedation criteria are entangled with respiratory/ARDS severity via P/F (PaO2/FiO2) ratio cutoffs of 150 (severe ARDS / NMBA-benefit boundary, ACURASYS/ROSE), 200 (Berlin moderate-ARDS boundary), and FiO2/PEEP bands, plus a tachypnea FR>22 weaning-readiness cutoff shared conceptually with qSOFA respiratory scoring.

Rule-count histogram by status: OK=19, DISCREPANCY=4, AMBIGUOUS=4 (total 27). By type: threshold=15, decision-tree=10, validation=2. By verification verdict: VERIFIED=7, DISCREPANCY=6, UNVERIFIABLE=8, NOT_APPLICABLE=6 (verify:false rules, mostly alert-aggregation and DB-constraint rules with no published clinical anchor).

load_bearing_rules.what: wired AMARELO pain criterion: VISUAL 4-6 or BPS 7-9 on two consecutive balances
wired VERMELHO pain criterion: VISUAL 7-10 or BPS 10-12 on two consecutive balances
wired VERMELHO criterion: any continuous sedative infusion >= drug-specific ml/h cutoff in last 4h; also sets active-drug label
wired AMARELO criterion: no >=50% morning sedation reduction 06:00-10:00, but operator-precedence bug means exactly-half reduction is NOT flagged as failure
picks first non-zero continuous sedative in fixed priority order (midazolam>propofol>fentanil>cetamina>dexmedetomidina) to label the active drug
production v3 alert aggregator: any VERMELHO criterion (c1,c8) trumps any AMARELO (c5,c7); a legacy alternate aggregator exists in code but is dead/unused
manual-pathway deep-sedation-with-low-ventilatory-support criterion; code allows PEEP==10 though its own descriptive rule text says PEEP<10
manual-pathway alert: >=3 of 6 criteria true -> VERMELHO, 1-2 -> AMARELO, 0 -> NEUTRO
v1 (superseded) alert aggregator using a different 4-criterion subset than v3
clinician-facing recommendation-text catalog spanning all 12 v3 criteria: RASS targets, NMBA/BNM stop criteria, PRIS lab surveillance cadence, and an opioid/analgesia drug ladder

systemic_issues.issue: Bound-order bug makes an unsatisfiable range (deep-sedation RASS check reversed to -3<=x<=-5, impossible)

Field-name bugs read nonexistent bare attributes (pins/peep/pf/fio2 instead of diurna_pins/diurna_peep/diurna_pf/diurna_fio2), silently defaulting to 0; plus impossible numeric comparisons (fio2>250, fr>250) that invert the documented intent
Absence-vs-presence inversion: code checks presence where docstring/intent requires absence (twice, in different rules)
Operator-precedence bug on 'reduce by at least half' check makes exact-half reduction pass as compliant instead of failing
Typo in a lab-order field name ('trigliceres' vs actual 'triglicerides') makes one leg of a PRIS-surveillance check permanently inert
Threshold drift between code and the model's own embedded _REGRAS description text (PEEP<10 documented vs PEEP<=10 implemented)

Three non-identical, never-reconciled alert-aggregation schemes (v3 wired, v1 legacy, manual) coexist for the same clinical concept with different criteria subsets and different vote thresholds — classic scoring drift across pathway versions
8 of 12 v3 predicate criteria are dead code (defined, never wired) — a large unused-logic surface carrying real bugs that never execute

Unit/anchor mismatch: institutional ml/h infusion-volume cutoffs have no clinical citation path (weight-based mcg/kg/min is the guideline unit); porting requires a drug-concentration table not present in this catalog

Display-label typo in a stored choice enum ('Midazolan' for key 'midazolam') and tests that assert against display labels rather than stored keys, meaning choice enforcement is effectively unverified at write time

open_questions: Which of the three alert-aggregation schemes (v3/v1/manual) should the v2 canonical sedation score inherit — none has a published anchor, so RATIFY is required per CONTRACTS.md.
Are ml/h infusion thresholds (RULE-SEDACAO-005, -010) worth ADOPT-CORRECTED with a drug-concentration table, or RETIRE in favor of a weight-based (mcg/kg/min) threshold model?
